claim_status: Backup-owner qualification template only; no named qualified backup, no owner identity verification, no role level review, no subsystem competence review, no representative path coverage, no recent review record, no availability record, no succession evidence, no recusal review, no inactive owner replacement, no CODEOWNERS reconciliation, no review-rule reconciliation, no stale privileged access review, no two-person control, no owner coverage claim, no release authority claim, no release promotion claim, no stable signing authority claim, no update trust claim, no supported-version changes claim, no security-disclosure authority claim, no irreversible migration approval claim, no legal approval claim, no incident closure authority claim, no production authority claim, no broad readiness claim, and no implementation claim.

candidate_scope.prohibited_placeholder_policy: Template records must not name placeholder people, title-only owners, aliases without qualification evidence, self-nominations without review, or private contact details; a real record must replace null fields with owner-reviewed evidence.

qualification_axes.required_evidence: Owner-reviewed role level for the named backup owner and scope.
Evidence that the named backup can review or operate the subsystem without relying on title-only ownership.
Representative protected paths and examples the backup can review.
Recent accepted review or equivalent owner-reviewed qualification record.
Availability window and escalation limits for the named backup owner.
Succession path if primary or backup becomes unavailable.
Recusal rule for conflicts, self-review, legal conflict, security embargo, or employment change.
Removal path for stale, compromised, unavailable, or unqualified backup ownership.
Emergency replacement procedure that keeps release, signing, disclosure, and incident closure unavailable until reviewed.

reconciliation_axes.required_evidence: CODEOWNERS routing matches the named qualified backup and protected paths.
Professional review rules name the correct reviewer scopes and independent review requirements.
Escalation policy reaches qualified humans without granting agents owner authority.
Support coverage is reconciled without implying supported security versions.
Signing separation stays owner-reviewed and does not grant stable signing authority from backup qualification alone.
Security-disclosure authority stays separate from backup-owner qualification.
Package/release ownership paths remain separate from qualification evidence.
CI and branch protection rules route reviews to qualified maintainers.
Service ownership and on-call paths are reconciled where the scope owns services.
Repository access grants are least-privilege and reviewed.
Stale privileged access is reviewed and removed before owner coverage is claimed.
Protected paths have primary and backup routing or stay blocked.
Primary-only paths are either remediated or explicitly kept blocked.
Blocked status is preserved for any scope that lacks a named qualified backup.
Single-owner residual risk is reviewed and accepted only by owners, not by a template.

two_person_control_axes.required_evidence: Two-person control remains required for stable signing.
Two-person control remains required for update trust changes.
Two-person control remains required for supported-version changes.
Two-person control remains required for security-disclosure decisions.
Two-person control remains required for irreversible profile migration approval.
Two-person control remains required for release promotion.
Two-person control remains required for legal approval.
Two-person control remains required for incident closure.

qualification_lifecycle.required_evidence: Select one scope from the checked build-critical scope list.
Name a real qualified backup candidate without placeholders or private contact details.
Verify identity and repository/account mapping through approved channels.
Record role level and responsibility limits.
Record representative path coverage and gaps.
Attach recent review record or accepted equivalent.
Record inactivity, removal, and emergency replacement rules.
Review repository access and stale privileged access.
Review ownerless protected paths and primary-only paths.
Owner either accepts the qualified backup evidence or keeps PB-019 blocked.

rejection_rules.condition: Candidate backup is a placeholder, title-only label, or null in a non-template record.
rejection_rules.outcome: Keep PB-019 blocked and reject owner coverage claim.

rejection_rules.condition: Candidate evidence is only self-nomination without independent owner review.

rejection_rules.outcome: Keep PB-019 blocked for that scope.
Keep escalation and owner coverage blocked.

rejection_rules.condition: Succession or recusal evidence is missing.
Inactivity, removal, or emergency replacement evidence is missing.
Review-rule or escalation-policy reconciliation is missing.
Stale privileged access is present or unreviewed.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject production authority and owner coverage claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Ownerless protected-path or primary-only path remains unreviewed.
Two-person control evidence is missing for release or incident authority paths.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject release authority, signing authority, disclosure authority, legal approval, and incident closure claims.

rejection_rules.condition: This checked no-claim template is cited as named backup ownership, owner coverage, two-person control, or authority evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject the claim and keep PB-019 blocked.

rejection_rules.condition: Backup qualification is used to claim release authority, signing authority, disclosure authority, legal approval, incident closure, production authority, broad readiness, or implementation approval.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject the claim and keep owner-only gates blocked.
